name: docker-k8s
description: Master containerization and orchestration with security-first approach. Expert in Docker multi-stage builds, Kubernetes zero-trust deployments, security hardening, GitOps workflows, and production-ready patterns for cloud-native applications. Includes 2025 best practices from CNCF and major cloud providers.
capabilities: Generate secure multi-stage Docker build files with non-root users and minimal attack surface
Create Kubernetes manifests with Pod Security Standards, RBAC, and network policies
Implement GitOps workflows with ArgoCD and Flux configurations
Configure service mesh with Istio for mTLS and traffic management
Set up Prometheus monitoring and alerting rules for security events
Apply CNCF best practices for production container deployments
author: Azeem-2
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only SKILL.md with code examples and patterns. No executable code exists. The static scanner flagged patterns found WITHIN markdown documentation examples (Dockerfiles, Kubernetes manifests, Python code snippets) as if they were real security issues. All flagged patterns are legitimate security best practices demonstrated in documentation context.
valueStatement: Container security requires careful attention to build patterns, network policies, and runtime configurations. This skill provides production-ready code patterns for secure multi-stage Docker builds, zero-trust Kubernetes deployments, service mesh integration, and GitOps workflows.
limitations: Does not execute Docker or kubectl commands directly
Does not connect to live Kubernetes clusters
Does not perform security scanning of existing images
Requires user to review and customize generated code for their environment

useCases.title: Production deployment patterns
useCases.description: Generate security-hardened Dockerfiles and Kubernetes manifests following industry best practices

useCases.title: Container security hardening
useCases.description: Implement zero-trust network policies, RBAC configurations, and runtime security controls

useCases.title: GitOps and observability setup
useCases.description: Configure ArgoCD applications with Prometheus monitoring and security alerting rules

promptTemplates.title: Secure Dockerfile
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a secure multi-stage Docker build for a Python FastAPI application with non-root user, minimal packages, and health checks

promptTemplates.title: K8s deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate Kubernetes deployment with Pod Security Standards, network policies, and secrets management for a production web application

promptTemplates.title: GitOps setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create ArgoCD application configuration with automated sync, self-heal, and notifications for a multi-environment deployment

promptTemplates.title: Service mesh
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure Istio VirtualService and DestinationRule with mTLS, circuit breakers, and retry policies for secure service communication

bestPractices: Always use multi-stage builds to minimize final image size and attack surface
Run containers as non-root users with minimal required capabilities
Apply Pod Security Standards and network policies to enforce zero-trust networking
Implement GitOps workflows with automated sync and drift detection
antiPatterns: Running containers as root user or with privileged access
Embedding secrets directly in container images or environment variables
Using latest tag for production images without digest pinning
Skipping network policies and relying on default cluster isolation

faq.question: Which Kubernetes versions are supported?
faq.answer: Patterns support Kubernetes 1.26+ including Pod Security Standards and current RBAC APIs.

faq.question: What cloud providers work with these patterns?
faq.answer: Patterns are cloud-agnostic. Examples include AWS EKS, Google GKE, Azure AKS, and DigitalOcean Kubernetes.

faq.question: Does this skill execute Docker commands?
faq.answer: No. This skill generates configuration files. Users must execute docker and kubectl commands themselves.

faq.question: How are secrets handled securely?
faq.answer: Patterns use Kubernetes Secrets with external secret management systems like Vault or cloud providers.

faq.question: Why do I need to customize the generated code?
faq.answer: Generated patterns are templates. Users must add their image registry, secrets, resource limits, and environment-specific values.

faq.question: How does this compare to other container tools?
faq.answer: This skill provides security-first patterns rather than generic templates. Focus is on hardening, zero-trust, and compliance.
